source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage to Crane/Lifting Device

actions_taken: Driller spaced out drill pipe and checked well
BSEE notified all rigs with same equipment
event.summary: On January 25, 2015 at 0100 hours, an actuator plate weighing 19 pounds fell approximately 90 feet from the top drive of the MDDM on the T.O. Deepwater Invictus rig. The bolts securing the upper actuator plate had sheared due to tight tolerances between the actuator plates and lever arms, allowing impact during drilling operations. No personnel were injured as all crew members were outside the Red Zone at the time of the incident. The equipment manufacturer identified the root cause as design tolerance issues and implemented corrective actions including machining and repositioning of components.
recommendations: Machine grind areas at risk for impact on plates and lever arms
Reposition fork assembly for lever arm to provide additional clearance
BSEE notified all rigs with same equipment to make appropriate corrections

hazards.name: Mechanical equipment with moving parts and high forces
Elevated work areas with potential for dropped objects

threats.name: Tight tolerances between actuator plates and lever arms causing impact during drilling
Bolt shearing due to mechanical impact and stress

consequences.name: Dropped object - actuator plate falling 90 feet to rig floor
Potential personnel injury or fatality from dropped object

controls.name: Red Zone restrictions - personnel exclusion from drill floor during certain operations
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to prevent personnel exposure to dropped objects
controls.lod_basis: Prevents personnel from being in impact zone during operations

supporting_text: At the time of the incident, all crew members were located outside of the area where the plate struck the floor due to the rigs 'Red Zone' restrictions
The 'Red Zone' is an area of the drill floor that personnel are instructed to stay clear of while certain operations are taking place

controls.name: Equipment design - actuator plate bolts and fastening system
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent bolt shearing and plate detachment
controls.lod_basis: Primary design barrier to prevent mechanical failure

supporting_text: It was discovered that the bolts for the upper actuator plate in the top drive had been sheared, allowing the plate to fall approximately 90 feet to the rig floor
MHWirth/Aker stated the tolerances were too tight between the actuator plates and the lever arms, which occasionally allowed the plates and arms to come into contact while drilling/rotating

controls.name: Operator monitoring and torque management - Driller observation of MDDM torque
controls.barrier_role: Administrative/operational control to detect and respond to equipment anomalies
controls.lod_basis: Early detection of equipment stress through operator monitoring

supporting_text: On January 25, 2015, the Driller noticed an increase in torque on the MDDM (Modular Derrick Drilling Machine) of the rig
As he reduced the torque, a loud noise was heard coming from the top drive, followed by an actuator plate falling to the rig floor

controls.name: Post-incident corrective actions - equipment modification and design improvement
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to eliminate root cause of bolt shearing
controls.lod_basis: Permanent design modification to prevent recurrence

supporting_text: MHWirth/Aker's recommended action to correct this issue was to machine grind the areas which were at risk for impact on the plates and the lever arms
They also slightly repositioned the fork assembly for the lever arm to give even more clearance
Once all of this work had been done to MHWirth/Aker's liking, they issued a new Certificate of Compliance for the equipment

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
